children.text: Just a century ago, cervical cancer killed more females in the U.S. than any other cancer. This is no longer the case. The Pap test is largely responsible for the seismic decline in cervical cancer diagnoses and deaths in the U.S.

Though worldwide cervical cancer is the 4th most diagnosed cancer in females, it fortunately doesn’t make the top 10 for women in the U.S. This can be equated to the access and education around perception methods. With tests, screenings, vaccinations, and lifestyle shifts, you have the tools to protect yourself from HPV, abnormal Paps, and cervical cancer!
Get a Pap test!
Heads up. Pap tests and HPV tests are separate tests, though often conducted at the same time. The vaccine is another way to protect yourself. These 3 are *not* mutually exclusive!
Prevention Methods
Did you know? There is no exam that tests males for HPV, but they are vectors, meaning they carry and pass the virus. This means vaccinations are key for both males & females!
So, what's going on down there?

Your cervix can bounce back from anything (just like you). Made from connective tissue, it is the lowest part of the uterus that connects to the vagina. It stretches slightly during menstruation and expands up to 10 centimeters during childbirth.
Despite what it sounds like, the transformation zone is not a ride at a county fair, it is the area that surrounds the opening of the cervix and where “
abnormal cells
” most commonly develop.
To clarify:
Not all abnormal cells lead to cancer, but all cancers are abnormal cells.

We all went through puberty and know that change means vulnerability. This is true even on a cellular level — cells are most vulnerable during periods of change. This makes the transformation zone at the cervix the most common place for abnormal cells to develop, which can, but do not always, lead to cancer.
There are two types of cells on the surface of the cervix: squamous and columnar. Throughout your lifetime
columnar cells
*transform* into squamous cells in this zone.
Columnar cells
are fragile and delicate and transform into tougher, more durable squamous cells in the transformation zone.
Normal Cells vs. Abnormal Cells
Columnar cells are fragile and delicate and transform into tougher, more durable squamous cells in the transformation zone.
Your body is made up of trillions of cells. Within each *normal* cell, there are genes that give directions on when to grow, multiply, group, and die.
*Abnormal* cells disobey these directions. Kind of like *that* guest at a party who insults the host, ignores the cocktail napkins, and doesn't leave when the party is over.
In other words, these cells multiply, divide, and behave in unplanned ways.
When cells get weird
Cervical dysplasia is the growth of abnormal cells on the surface of the cervix. There is a spectrum to dysplasia

— an increase in cell abnormality determines where dysplasia is classified on the spectrum (mild to severe). When abnormal cells uncontrollably mobilize beyond the cervical tissue barrier they become cervical cancer.
The number-one risk factor for developing cervical cancer is infection with a high-risk strain of
HPV
, which sets the stage for the cells to get weird.
Did you know?
Most HPV is wiped out by a healthy immune system within two years.

Referred to as the “common cold” of the sexually active world, HPV, aka the human papillomavirus, is the most common sexually transmitted infection worldwide. The Centers for Disease Control states that almost *all*
sexually active
people will contract at least one HPV strain at some point in their lives.
Heads up.
HPV is transmitted via intercourse, but also through skin-to-skin contact in the genital region

A virus is a small infectious agent that replicates in living cells. A strain is a certain biological form of a microorganism. Viruses are like families and strains are like the cousins within the extended family — related but not exactly identical.
It's a fact:

items.snippet: The 3 most dangerous strains of HPV are 16+18+45. If you have one of these strains you must be diligent.
Females over the age of 40 are the group with the fastest rising rates of STI contraction.
Your risk of contracting HPV is 6x greater by having 3+ partners in your lifetime.
items.title: 6x

items.snippet: Ages 25 and under are at peak risk of contracting cancer-causing HPV, but also most likely to clear the virus.
of the most common type of cervical cancer (squamous), is caused by strains 16 + 18.
strains of HPV affect humans but most are not dangerous.

children.text: Did you know?

High-risk strains can lead to cervical dysplasia and cancer. This includes cervical cancer, anal cancer, throat cancer, and vulvar cancer. The extent of the correlation between high-risk HPV and cervical cancer is greater than that between smoking and lung cancer. Low-risk strains can lead to genital and anal warts.
Just like a toothbrush, a good night’s sleep, and a strong wifi connection, you *need* the HPV vaccine.

cells: Gardasil-9 is the FDA approved HPV vaccine that protects you against the most harmful high-risk and low-risk strains of HPV.
Gardasil-9 was tested on 15,000 people prior to FDA approval and found no scientific evidence suggesting links between adverse symptoms and the vaccine.
The HPV vaccine helps protect against certain strains of HPV (both high-risk and low-risk).

The CDC recommends administering the vaccine as early as age 11 before most people are sexually active. You can get the vaccine up to age 45. If you haven't had the vaccine, talk to your provider to see if its right for you!
Generally the vaccine is administered in 3 separate shots and it takes 6 months to get all 3.
I didn't get all three shots. What do I do?
Talk to your provider. Some research has found that there is no reduction in protection seen among females who missed doses two and three of the HPV vaccine.

children.text: HPV is an *our* problem, not a *her* problem
Only people with cervixes can get cervical cancer but
HPV affects all of us
Heads up.
Each time you engage with a new sexual partner (or a partner you are not in a monogamous relationship with) you are at risk for being exposed to a new strain of HPV.

Males are not tested for HPV but they are carriers and transmit the virus. They can also suffer from the ramifications of the untreated virus. It is estimated that HPV causes 9,000 new cancers in males in the U.S. every year, including nearly all anal cancers, 63% of penile cancers, and 70% of throat cancers.

People talk smack about the Pap because it can feel invasive, uncomfortable, and straight-up awkward. But the rise of this easy-to-perform, inexpensive, and highly-reliable test has made a colossal impact on the decline of cervical cancer incidence.

The Pap test is named after Greek immigrant, Dr. Papanikolaou. He rightly believed and proved that most forms of cervical cancer begin with an infection and the changes in the cells that ultimately become cancer cells progress in a predictable fashion over time. He is the pioneer behind early cervical cancer detection.
In a Pap test, cells are taken from the cervix using a speculum and examined under a microscope. Pap tests detect abnormal cells.

asset.originalFilename: Pap Smear vs. Cervical Cancer - Abnormal Pap Smear.png

children.text: Abnormal Pap
An abnormal Pap is a broad categorization that can mean a bunch of different things — it can indicate HPV, a different kind of infection, or unusual cells on the cervix.
Your Care Provider may follow up with additional testing to get more information on what’s going on down there.
Post-Pap testing
My results are abnormal?
Heads up. Abnormal Pap results are on a spectrum. Talk to your provider about where you fall and what kind of follow up testing you need.
My results are normal?
Did you know? Abnormal cells can be removed before they develop into cervical cancer and sometimes the cells will spontaneously revert back to normal on their own.
No one-size-fits-all solutions
So your bestie says they were told they only needed a Pap every 5 years but your mom insists it should be yearly? What’s the deal?

According to the latest guidelines from the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG), females 21-29 should get a Pap test every 3 years and females 30-65 should get a Pap test and HPV test every 5 years (this is called co-testing), with more frequent testing when results are abnormal. These guidelines are based on both large population data and cost-benefit analysis. In layperson’s terms, this means they are general guidelines.

At Tia, we believe that no two patients are the same and so the care we provide is individualized and accounts for the factors that make you *you.* Based on a range of factors from sexual behavior to a history of HPV to lifestyle factors that may weaken your immune system, your Care Provider at Tia may recommend more frequent Pap testing.
Beyond getting Pap tests and the
HPV vaccine

, there are things you can do in your daily life that support your immune system, and in turn, can help you fight off viruses and disease. These lifestyle-based recommendations are all backed-by-science and 100% in your control.
Did you know?
You can get your HPV vaccine at the Tia Clinic. Ready to book?
Click here.
You’re unique. Your care should be too.
At Tia, we provide personalized care that spans
ten specialties
, working to prevent disease and uncover root cause issues. From everyday wellness to complex concerns, our holistic approach puts you at the center of it all.
Questions about services?
1 Care Plan. 10 specialties. Fully connected.

Gynecology and sexual care, primary care, perimenopause & menopause, hormonal health, fertility & post partum support, weight & metabolic health, longevity, mental health, acupuncture, and skin care together under one roof—and in one coordinated Care Plan. No more bouncing between providers who never communicate. With Tia, every part of your health is treated as part of the whole, so you get care that actually works together—just like your body.
Gynecology and Sexual Health

From annual exams to navigating perimenopause and finding the right birth control, our expert providers take a holistic approach to care. We go beyond symptoms to see the bigger picture, offering ongoing support for concerns that need more than a one-time solution.
Our gynecological services include:

items: Well-woman exams (including pap tests & breast exams)
Fertility care, from assessment to conception support
Vaginal, vulvar, or urinary symptoms (including STI testing & treatment)

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Feeling low energy, mood swings, or sudden changes that do not feel like you? This new assessment helps connect the dots so you get clarity, a personalized plan, and the support to feel like yourself again.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.
Primary Care

We believe in preventing issues before they start so you can live as many healthy, vibrant years as possible. Our providers specialize in women’s health and partner with you to understand your unique needs—from preventative care to chronic condition management.
Our primary care services include:

items: Acute care for common illnesses & more

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.
Perimenopause & Menopause

Your hormones don’t just “wind down” during perimenopause and menopause, they shift in ways that can impact everything from mood and sleep to weight, libido, and how you feel in your body. Our clinicians take a whole person approach to these changes, looking at the interplay between hormones, lifestyle, and symptoms to understand what’s really going on. We then build a personalized Care Plan to help you manage symptoms, protect long term health, and navigate this transition feeling informed, supported, and in control.

items: Navigate hormonal shifts clinically and naturally
Conventional and integrative treatments like HRT, nutrition, supplements, lifestyle support and acupuncture

children.text: Hormonal Health

Your hormones are powerful messengers that influence everything from your mood and energy to digestion, skin, and sexual health. When they’re out of balance, the symptoms can feel confusing, disconnected, or hard to pinpoint. Our clinicians take a comprehensive look at your hormonal ecosystem, exploring patterns across your cycle, lifestyle, stress, sleep, and labs to uncover what’s really driving your symptoms. Then we create a personalized plan to rebalance hormones, ease discomfort, and help you feel more in tune with your body every day.

items: Get answers for irregular cycle, bloating & cramps, or PMS
Understand how your hormones impact your whole health
A collaborative care team that connects the dots
Care designed for women at every life stage

children.text: Fertility & Postpartum Support

We're here for you, from questions about your fertility to trying to conceive, to navigating postpartum recovery. We provide expert care for your body and mind, without the pressure to pursue procedures or timelines that aren't right for you.

items: Get clarity on ovulation, cycle timing, and fertility challenges
Support for conception, pregnancy, and life after birth
Guidance for physical and emotional changes in the postpartum period
Evidence based support for breastfeeding, pelvic recovery, and mood

children.text: Weight Management & Metabolic Health

Your weight, energy, and metabolic health are deeply interconnected, so treating them in isolation rarely works. Our clinicians look at the full picture of what is happening in your body, from hormones and insulin sensitivity to nutrition, stress, sleep, and lifestyle patterns. By understanding how these factors shape your metabolism, we create a personalized plan that supports sustainable weight management, improved energy, and long term wellness tailored to your whole self.

items: For weight changes, insulin resistance, and metabolic health concerns
Personalized to your body and goals
Tools like GLP-1s, nutrition, and lifestyle when appropriate

children.text: Longevity

Longevity isn’t just about living longer, it’s about feeling better as you age. Your daily habits, hormones, metabolic health, and stress levels all influence how your body performs over time. Our clinicians look at the full picture, from labs and lifestyle to sleep, nutrition, and movement patterns, to understand what supports or accelerates aging in your unique system. Then we develop a personalized plan that helps you build strength, protect cognitive and metabolic health, and stay vibrant and resilient for the long haul.

items: Identify early markers that impact long term health and vitality
Personalized strategies for sleep, stress, nutrition, and movement
Care for your heart, brain, and beyond

children.text: Mental Health

Your mind and body are inextricably linked—so why should they be treated separately? Our expert clinicians take a comprehensive look at what’s going on with both—including physiological symptoms, aka how mental health feels in your body—to create a holistic Care Plan tailored to your whole self.
Our mental health offerings include:

items: Referrals to trusted partners for individual, couples, or family therapy

children.text: Offered at:
Tia Virtual Clinic
Acupuncture

Acupuncture, a science-backed Traditional Asian Medicine practice, offers a natural, holistic approach to addressing a wide range of concerns—from anxiety to fertility. Tia acupuncturists are women’s health experts who complement your care with treatments proven to work.
We offer acupuncture for these concerns
(and more!)
Skin Care
Whether you're addressing a specific concern or just want to feel more confident in your skin, you’ll get trusted care from licensed Tia providers using science-backed, proven solutions.
We offer a range of custom medical and aesthetic skin care services, including:
Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.
Ready to get the care you deserve—exceptional, compassionate, and all in one place?

Your health isn’t one thing, it’s everything. Whether you’re managing symptoms, navigating a life transition, or simply trying to understand your body better, Tia connects the dots so you don’t have to. Explore our services, meet your care team, and take the next step toward feeling informed, supported, and in control of your whole health. Ready when you are.

children.text: Physical Activity
Physical activity stimulates the immune system. Strong immune systems are better at fighting off viruses and may support the body in fighting off cervical cancer.
Get moving!
Vitamin D

80-90% of females are vitamin D deficient. Vitamin D helps with the stimulation of antibody formation. There is clinical research showing that vitamin D supplementation can lead to the regression of mild dysplasia. 20 minutes in the sun 3x a week can make all the difference.
Get your vitamin D!
No Smoking
Smoking limits the immune system's ability to fight off HPV. Females who smoke are about twice as likely as non-smokers to get cervical cancer.
Skip the cigs!
Stress
Limit chronic stress. Or better yet, learn how to modulate the impact of allostatic load on your immune system. Does this sound like a different language?
Take the stress quiz!
Sleep

Sleep is absolutely essential to the body's cellular health. During sleep — especially deep, slow-wave sleep — the body goes to work to repair damaged cells and DNA, promoting healthy new cell growth, fortifying and strengthening the immune system.
Sleep is essential!
Nutrition

We're all about nutrient delivery and we think of #FoodasMedicine. Diets high in carotenoids, foods with red, yellow, and orange pigment (think squash, oranges, apricots) and vitamin C may help reduce cancer risk.
Nutrition is vital.
Now you know what you need to know!

We created this guide because we believe information about your body and health should be super accessible, science-backed, and maybe even a little fun. We believe science need not be confusing and that choice in healthcare begins with education!
